context: Tests whether the agent uses the ElevenLabs transcribe skill's realtime URL streaming mode correctly: using the --url flag for live streams, the --partials flag for partial transcript display, --quiet to produce clean stdout for piping, and passing these through the skill's shell script entry point. Also checks that the agent documents the realtime model in design notes.

checklist.name: Shell script entry point
checklist.description: monitor_stream.sh invokes transcribe.sh (the skill's script) rather than calling Python directly or using curl to the API

checklist.name: URL streaming flag
checklist.description: Uses --url flag (not --realtime, which is for local files) when passing the stream URL to the transcription command

checklist.name: URL passed as argument
checklist.description: The stream URL argument from monitor_stream.sh is forwarded to the --url parameter of the transcription command

checklist.name: Partials flag wired up
checklist.description: When --show-partials is passed to monitor_stream.sh, the --partials flag is forwarded to the transcription command

checklist.name: Quiet mode for piping
checklist.description: Passes -q or --quiet flag so that only transcript text goes to stdout (status messages suppressed), enabling clean downstream piping

checklist.name: Realtime model noted
checklist.description: design_notes.md mentions scribe_v2_realtime (or 'realtime model') as the model used for URL/stream transcription

checklist.name: Distinguishes URL vs batch mode
checklist.description: design_notes.md explains that --url triggers realtime streaming (not batch file transcription), and why this enables low latency

checklist.name: API key reference
checklist.description: Script or design notes reference ELEVENLABS_API_KEY as a required environment variable

checklist.name: Does NOT use --realtime for URL
checklist.description: Script does NOT pass --realtime flag when using --url (--realtime is only for local files, not live URL streams)
